revision.text:
'''お熊甲祭'''（おくまかぶとまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]]中島町宮前に鎮座する[[久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社]]（くまかぶとあらかしひこじんじゃ）の例祭。毎年[[9月20日]]に行われることから「'''二十日祭'''（はつかまつり・はつかさい）」ともいう。「'''熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事'''」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（1981年）されている。
== 概要 == 高さ20メートルもある[[羅紗]]製の真紅の枠旗（大幟）が競い合って練り回るのがみどころ。
久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社は、中島町内にある[[熊木村|熊木]]、[[中島町中島|中島]]、[[西岸村|西岸]]、[[豊川村 (石川県)|豊川]]の4集落の各所に鎮座する[[末社]]19社の総本社で、例祭の日にこれら末社の[[神輿]]が本社に大集合する寄合祭りである。夜が白みかける頃より[[猿田彦]]を先導に列をなし、昼頃までに全末社が本社に繰り込む。次いで祭典に移り、[[鉦]]、[[太鼓]]の音が響く中を、あでやかな衣裳を身に着けた猿田彦が境内を乱舞する。午後からは枠旗、鉦、太鼓の行列を従えた本社の神輿を先頭にして加茂原の[[御旅所]]に入り、枠旗を地上すれすれまで傾ける妙技「島田くずし」を披露<ref>[[佐藤和彦]], [[保田博通]]編『祭りの事典』[[東京堂出版]]、321頁。</ref>するなど、各社競い合って練り回り、修祓の儀をもって幕を閉じる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『カラー 日本のまつり』山と渓谷社編、[[山と渓谷社]]、1967年。
== 関連項目 == * [[中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館]]（石川県七尾市中島町）
== 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おくまかふとまつり}} [[Category:石川県の祭り]]

revision.text:
'''お熊甲祭'''（おくまかぶとまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]]中島町宮前に鎮座する[[久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社]]（くまかぶとあらかしひこじんじゃ）の例祭。毎年[[9月20日]]に行われることから「'''二十日祭'''（はつかまつり・はつかさい）」ともいう。「'''熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事'''」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（1981年）されている。
== 概要 == 高さ20メートルもある[[羅紗]]製の真紅の枠旗（大幟）が競い合って練り回るのがみどころ。
久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社は、中島町内にある[[熊木村|熊木]]、[[中島町中島|中島]]、[[西岸村|西岸]]、[[豊川村 (石川県)|豊川]]の4集落の各所に鎮座する[[末社]]19社の総本社で、例祭の日にこれら末社の[[神輿]]が本社に大集合する寄合祭りである。夜が白みかける頃より[[猿田彦]]を先導に列をなし、昼頃までに全末社が本社に繰り込む。次いで祭典に移り、[[鉦]]、[[太鼓]]の音が響く中を、あでやかな衣裳を身に着けた猿田彦が境内を乱舞する。午後からは枠旗、鉦、太鼓の行列を従えた本社の神輿を先頭にして加茂原の[[御旅所]]に入り、枠旗を地上すれすれまで傾ける妙技「島田くずし」を披露<ref>[[佐藤和彦]], [[保田博通]]編『祭りの事典』[[東京堂出版]]、321頁。</ref>するなど、各社競い合って練り回り、修祓の儀をもって幕を閉じる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『カラー 日本のまつり』山と渓谷社編、[[山と渓谷社]]、1967年。
== 関連項目 == * [[中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館]]（石川県七尾市中島町）
== 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}}

revision.text:
'''お熊甲祭'''（おくまかぶとまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]][[中島町 (石川県)|中島町]]宮前に鎮座する[[久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社]]（くまかぶとあらかしひこじんじゃ）の例祭。毎年[[9月20日]]に行われることから「'''二十日祭'''（はつかまつり・はつかさい）」ともいう<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=お熊甲祭＜国指定重要無形民俗文化財＞ |url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html |website=七尾市 |access-date=2022-10-15 |language=ja |last=七尾市}}</ref>。「'''熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事'''」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（[[1981年]]）されている<ref name=":0" />。
== 概要 == 久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社は、中島町内にある[[熊木村|熊木]]、[[中島町中島|中島]]、[[西岸村|西岸]]、[[豊川村 (石川県)|豊川]]の4集落の各所に鎮座する[[末社]]19社の総本社である。'''お熊甲祭'''では、例祭の日に19の末社の[[神輿]]が本社に大集合する<ref name=":0" />。早朝から[[猿田彦]]を先導に列をなし、昼頃までに全末社が本社に繰り込む<ref name=":0" />。次いで祭典に移り、[[鉦]]、[[太鼓]]の音が響く中を、あでやかな衣裳を身に着けた猿田彦が境内を乱舞する<ref name=":0" />。午後からは枠旗、鉦、太鼓の行列を従えた本社の神輿を先頭にして加茂原の[[御旅所]]に入り<ref name=":0" />、枠旗を地上すれすれまで傾ける妙技「島田くずし」を披露する<ref name=":0" /><ref>[[佐藤和彦]], [[保田博通]]編『祭りの事典』[[東京堂出版]]、321頁。</ref>など、各社競い合って練り回り、修祓の儀をもって幕を閉じる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

/* 外部リンク */
revision.text:
'''お熊甲祭'''（おくまかぶとまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]][[中島町 (石川県)|中島町]]宮前に鎮座する[[久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社]]（くまかぶとあらかしひこじんじゃ）の例祭。毎年[[9月20日]]に行われることから「'''二十日祭'''（はつかまつり・はつかさい）」ともいう<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=お熊甲祭＜国指定重要無形民俗文化財＞ |url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html |website=七尾市 |access-date=2022-10-15 |language=ja |last=七尾市}}</ref>。長大な枠旗を舁くという極めて珍しい特色などから<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=重要無形民俗文化財|other=「詳細解説」参照}}</ref>、「'''熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事'''」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（[[1981年]]）されている<ref name=":0" /><ref name="database"/>。
== 概要 == 久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社は、中島町内にある[[熊木村|熊木]]、[[中島町中島|中島]]、[[西岸村|西岸]]、[[豊川村 (石川県)|豊川]]の4集落の各所に鎮座する[[末社]]19社の総本社である{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。'''お熊甲祭'''では、例祭の日に19の末社の[[神輿]]が、高さ20メートルにもなる深紅の羅紗製大枠旗を従えて本社に大集合する<ref name=":0" />。早朝から[[猿田彦]]を先導に列をなし、昼頃までに全末社が本社に繰り込む<ref name=":0" />。次いで祭典に移り、[[鉦]]、[[太鼓]]の音が響く中を、あでやかな衣裳を身に着けた猿田彦が境内を乱舞する<ref name=":0" />。午後からは枠旗、鉦、太鼓の行列を従えた本社の神輿を先頭にして加茂原の[[御旅所]]に入り<ref name=":0" />、枠旗を地上すれすれまで傾ける妙技「島田くずし」を披露する<ref name=":0" /><ref>[[佐藤和彦]], [[保田博通]]編『祭りの事典』[[東京堂出版]]、321頁。</ref>など、各社競い合って練り回り、修祓の儀をもって祭りの幕を閉じる{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。
== げんぞ参り == [[能登国|能登]]の各地には、[[氏子]]入りした新嫁が[[氏神]]に宮参りをする「[[げんぞまいり]]」と呼ばれる風習が伝えられている{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。'''お熊甲祭'''においては、祭りの裏側でひそやかに新嫁の初参りと社会的承認が行われた{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。すなわち、次の祭までの一年間に氏子外から嫁に来た新嫁は、祭りの当日に[[島田髷]]を結って盛装し、母親を同伴して久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社に参詣する{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。このとき、祭の人足に出た新郎（婿）の弁当を必ず重箱に詰めて持参し、周囲の人達にもこれを振る舞う{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。帰宅の際には予め注文しておいた饅頭を空になった重箱に詰めて持ち帰り、親戚や隣近所に贈った{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}という。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|ref= harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館]]（石川県七尾市中島町） * [[和倉温泉お祭り会館]]
== 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} [https://okumakabuto.jp/ {{DEFAULTSORT:おくまかふとまつり}}公式サイト] [[Category:七尾市の祭り]] [[Category:9月]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
'''お熊甲祭'''（おくまかぶとまつり）は、[[石川県]][[七尾市]][[中島町 (石川県)|中島町]]宮前に鎮座する[[久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社]]（くまかぶとあらかしひこじんじゃ）の例祭。毎年[[9月20日]]に行われることから「'''二十日祭'''（はつかまつり・はつかさい）」ともいう<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=お熊甲祭＜国指定重要無形民俗文化財＞ |url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html |website=七尾市 |access-date=2022-10-15 |language=ja |last=七尾市}}</ref>。長大な枠旗を舁くという極めて珍しい特色などから<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=重要無形民俗文化財|other=「詳細解説」参照}}</ref>、「'''熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事'''」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（[[1981年]]）されている<ref name=":0" /><ref name="database"/>。
== 概要 == 久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社は、中島町内にある[[熊木村|熊木]]、[[中島町中島|中島]]、[[西岸村|西岸]]、[[豊川村 (石川県)|豊川]]の4集落の各所に鎮座する[[末社]]19社の総本社である{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。'''お熊甲祭'''では、例祭の日に19の末社の[[神輿]]が、高さ20メートルにもなる深紅の羅紗製大枠旗を従えて本社に大集合する<ref name=":0" />。早朝から[[猿田彦]]を先導に列をなし、昼頃までに全末社が本社に繰り込む<ref name=":0" />。次いで祭典に移り、[[鉦]]、[[太鼓]]の音が響く中を、あでやかな衣裳を身に着けた猿田彦が境内を乱舞する<ref name=":0" />。午後からは枠旗、鉦、太鼓の行列を従えた本社の神輿を先頭にして加茂原の[[御旅所]]に入り<ref name=":0" />、枠旗を地上すれすれまで傾ける妙技「島田くずし」を披露する<ref name=":0" /><ref>[[佐藤和彦]], [[保田博通]]編『祭りの事典』[[東京堂出版]]、321頁。</ref>など、各社競い合って練り回り、修祓の儀をもって祭りの幕を閉じる{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。
== げんぞ参り == [[能登国|能登]]の各地には、[[氏子]]入りした新嫁が[[氏神]]に宮参りをする「[[げんぞまいり]]」と呼ばれる風習が伝えられている{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。'''お熊甲祭'''においては、祭りの裏側でひそやかに新嫁の初参りと社会的承認が行われた{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。すなわち、次の祭までの一年間に氏子外から嫁に来た新嫁は、祭りの当日に[[島田髷]]を結って盛装し、母親を同伴して久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社に参詣する{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。このとき、祭の人足に出た新郎（婿）の弁当を必ず重箱に詰めて持参し、周囲の人達にもこれを振る舞う{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}。帰宅の際には予め注文しておいた饅頭を空になった重箱に詰めて持ち帰り、親戚や隣近所に贈った{{sfn|山と渓谷社|1967|p=158}}という。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|ref= harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館]]（石川県七尾市中島町） * [[和倉温泉お祭り会館]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://okumakabuto.jp/ 公式サイト]（熊甲祭奉賛会） * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事}} [[Category:七尾市の祭り]] [[Category:9月]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]]

revision.comment: Bot作業依頼#Cite webの和書引数追加

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109598617]]
Wikidata name: Okuma Kabuto Festival
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|qq=|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|qq=|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|qq=|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|qq=|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|qq=|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|qq=|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|qq=|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|qq=|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

Wikidata name: Okuma Kabuto Festival
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

Wikidata name: Okuma Kabuto Festival
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109598617]]
Wikidata name: Okuma Kabuto Festival
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima]] district of Miyamae. Held every year on [[:en:September 20|September 20]], it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the [[:en:Nakajima, Ishikawa|Nakajima Town]] villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the [[:en:Otabisho|Otabisho]] at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In [[:en:Noto Province|Noto Province]], newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109598617]] Wikidata name: Okuma Kabuto Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima}} district of Miyamae. Held every year on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}}, it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}
Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
== Overview ==
Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima Town}} villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" />
In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabisho}} at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />
== Genzo Pilgrimage ==
In {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province}}, newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Nakajima Festival Archives|ja|中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima Festival Archives|1=Nakajima Festival Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364345}} * {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|lt=Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|lt=Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://okumakabuto.jp/ Official Website] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|お熊甲祭|version=101358483|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:お熊甲祭]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]]
'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima}} district of Miyamae. Held every year on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20}}, it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}
Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />
== Overview ==
Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima Town}} villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" />
In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabisho}} at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />
== Genzo Pilgrimage ==
In {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province}}, newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="Note"}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Items == * {{ill|Nakajima Festival Archives|ja|中島お祭り資料館・お祭り伝承館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima Festival Archives|1=Nakajima Festival Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364345}} * {{ill|Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|lt=Wakura Onsen Festival Hall|lt=Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://okumakabuto.jp/ Official Website] * {{国指定文化財等データベース|302|61|Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival}} * {{文化遺産オンライン|199736|Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival}}
{{translated page|ja|お熊甲祭|version=101358483|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:お熊甲祭]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:お熊甲祭]]

'''Okuma Kabuto Festival''' (お熊甲祭) is the annual [[Reisai|regular festival]] of {{ill|Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja|久麻加夫都阿良加志比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, located in {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima|1=Nakajima, Ishikawa}} district of Miyamae. Held every year on {{ill|September 20|en|September 20|WD=Q2892|lt=September 20|1=September 20}}, it is also referred to as the '''Hatsuka Festival''' (二十日祭, Hatsuka Matsuri or Hatsuka Sai).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Okuma Kabuto Festival: A Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/koryu-s/event/9gatsu/okumakabuto-.html|website=Nanao City|access-date=2022-10-15|language=ja}}</ref> It is one of the {{ill|Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja|七尾四大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Major Festivals of Nanao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along with the {{ill|Seihakusai|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}}, {{ill|Mukōda Fire Festival|ja|向田の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukōda Fire Festival|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|ja|石崎奉燈祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizaki Hōtō Festival|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.nanao.lg.jp/kikaku-s/shise/sesaku/1sogokekaku/honpen/documents/kihonkeikaku_1_4_5.pdf|title=Basic Plan: Chapter 1, Arts and Culture|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Nanao City|page=88}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="Note"|text=The Seihakusai, Ishizaki Hōtō Festival, {{ill|Nanao Gion Festival|ja|七尾祇園祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Gion Festival|1=Nanao Gion Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353501}}, and {{ill|Nanao Port Festival|ja|七尾港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanao Port Festival|1=Nanao Port Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353495}} are sometimes listed as the four major festivals.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://yamaguchi-ev.com/ishikawa-event/1692/|title=Events in Ishikawa Prefecture in May|access-date=2024-08-04|publisher=Yamaguchi Corporation}}</ref>}}

Due to its unique use of massive framed banners (''waku-hata'') measuring up to 20 meters, the festival is designated as the '''Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival''' (熊甲二十日祭の枠旗行事), a [[Nationally Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] since 1981.<ref name="database">{{国指定文化財等データベース2|name=Framed Banner Procession of the Kumakabuto Hatsuka Festival|register=302|item=61|year=1981|jyear=昭和56|month=1|date=21|cat1=Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property}}</ref><ref name=":0" />

== Overview == 

Kumakabuto Arakashi Hiko Shrine is the main shrine of 19 subordinate shrines located throughout the {{ill|Nakajima, Ishikawa|en|Nakajima, Ishikawa|WD=Q6960171|lt=Nakajima Town|1=Nakajima, Ishikawa}} villages of {{ill|Kumaki|ja|熊木村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumaki|1=Kumaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567906}}, {{ill|Nakajima|ja|中島町中島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakajima|1=Nakajimamachi Nakajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17175259}}, {{ill|Saigishi|ja|西岸村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigishi|1=Nishigishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627976}}, and {{ill|Toyokawa|ja|豊川村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa|1=Q11633634|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633634}}.<ref name=":0" /> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', portable shrines (''mikoshi'') from all 19 subordinate shrines, each accompanied by vibrant red woolen-framed banners up to 20 meters tall, gather at the main shrine.<ref name=":0" /> The day begins with a procession led by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, with all the subordinate shrines arriving at the main shrine by noon.<ref name=":0" /> Following a ceremony, Sarutahiko performs a spirited dance in the shrine grounds to the sound of gongs and drums.<ref name=":0" /> 

In the afternoon, the main shrine’s ''mikoshi'' leads the procession to the {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=Otabisho|1=Otabisho}} at Kamohara. Here, a dramatic banner maneuver called ''Shimada Kuzushi,'' which involves lowering the framed banners to ground level, is performed.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite book|title=Dictionary of Festivals|editor={{ill|Kazuhiko Sato|ja|佐藤和彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuhiko Sato|1=Kazuhiko Satō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383828}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|page=321}}</ref> Each shrine competes in displays of skill before the festival concludes with a purification ritual.<ref name=":0" />

== Genzo Pilgrimage == 

In {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, newlywed women traditionally participate in a ritual called ''Genzo Mairi,'' where they visit the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} shrine to gain community acceptance.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref> During the '''Okuma Kabuto Festival''', newlywed women from outside the parish community secretly perform their first shrine visit alongside their mothers. The women dress in formal attire, carrying tiered lunch boxes filled with food to be shared with the shrine attendees. Upon returning home, these lunch boxes are filled with pre-ordered sweets, which are distributed to relatives and neighbors.<ref>{{cite book|editor= 山と渓谷社|title= カラー 日本のまつり|publisher= [[山と渓谷社]]|year= 1967|page=158}}</ref>
